summary: Machine-readable Dory CLI capability map for non-interactive coding agents.

defaults.promptPolicy: commands should not prompt unless an operation explicitly needs --apply or user credentials
defaults.destructivePolicy: run dry-run or read-only commands first; require explicit --apply/--include-volumes and exact --confirm TARGET acknowledgement for Dory-owned delete, restore, and component-removal operations
defaults.hostToolPolicy: doryd reconciles bundled docker, docker compose, dory, and Docker context integration automatically while it is running; the Kubernetes component adds kubectl; do not require dory install for first use

exitCodes.1: runtime unhealthy, probe failed, or requested operation could not complete

recommendedRecoveryLoop: Run dory support bundle --json when filing an issue or collecting a shareable evidence pack.
Run dory doctor --json before modifying runtime state.
If a result includes an action, prefer the matching dory repair TARGET --json dry run.
Only add --apply when the user or task has authorized a write.
After repair, re-run the smallest relevant doctor group.

commands.notes: Passes through to docker against Dory's socket.
Ordered app, doryd, VM, guest-agent, data-mount, network, dockerd, host socket/context, and optional Kubernetes truth with timings and bounded repair ownership.
Writes a redacted support zip and returns schema dev.dory.support.bundle v1 with the shareable path.
Manual recovery only. doryd keeps docker, docker compose, and dory current in ~/.dory/bin without Docker Desktop or admin access. The Kubernetes component adds kubectl.

commands.invoke: dory component list|install|update|verify [COMPONENT] | dory component remove COMPONENT --confirm COMPONENT
destructiveRequires: remove requires --confirm with the exact component ID
commands.notes: Manages signed optional payloads on the selected Dory data drive. Desktop distributions add linux-desktop automatically. Removal preserves workload data.

repair all --apply never restarts the engine (it stays data-plane safe and warns to run `dory repair engine --restart-engine` instead). Bouncing the engine stops running containers, so `dory repair engine --apply` and the dockerd escalation require the explicit --restart-engine flag; without it they report the restart is available but disruptive and exit cleanly. Actions that stop the engine are marked dataPlaneDisruptive=true.

commands.invoke: dory network --json [--active] [--probe HOST[:PORT]] | dory network authorization-plan --json | dory network authorize --json --dry-run
commands.notes: Classifies DNS, registry, proxy, port, and local-domain failures. authorization-plan and authorize use doryd's live resolver/pf/TLS/low-port plan; authorize defaults to --dry-run and requires explicit --apply plus admin privileges for writes.

commands.invoke: dory network corporate status --json | dory network corporate plan --file PROFILE.json | dory network corporate apply --file PROFILE.json
commands.notes: status and plan are read-only. apply and disable change ownership-tracked Docker client and managed-guest settings; apply may restart dockerd with live-restore when the effective digest changes. Probe evidence names DNS, route, proxy, and CA provenance.

commands.invoke: dory idle status --json | dory idle history --json

commands.notes: Routes through dorydctl when available; wake/sleep are explicit state-changing commands.

commands.invoke: dory machine ls|create|start|stop|snapshot|exec ... | dory machine delete NAME --confirm NAME | dory machine restore NAME SNAPSHOT --confirm NAME
destructiveRequires: restore requires --confirm NAME and creates a retained pre-restore snapshot
commands.notes: Requires dorydctl and Dory's daemon-managed VM machine runtime; use `dory machine exec NAME --json -- CMD` for schema dev.dory.machine.exec v1. Managed Debian, Ubuntu, and Kali Xfce machines require their matching desktop component; Alpine headless machines require linux-machines. Set DORY_LEGACY_DOCKER_MACHINES=1 only for old container-backed machine inspection.

commands.invoke: dory machine backup status [NAME] | schedule NAME [--frequency hourly|daily|weekly] [--keep N] [--verify-every N] | run NAME | remove NAME
commands.notes: Daemon-owned local recovery bundles. Every run re-import-verifies the archive; the first and configured periodic runs boot a disposable verifier. Snapshot capture may briefly stop a running source machine. Retention affects only scheduler-owned snapshots and archives; no managed offsite/S3 backup is claimed.
Interactive terminal attach; use machine lifecycle/status commands for automation.
--print-command is non-interactive and does not start the tunnel.
Host discovery is supported. Attach, detach, and replay are unavailable and fail closed until the guest USB/IP RPC and physical qualification exist.
Read-only inspection of candidate/preflight/smoke/rollback/recovery state. Recovery returns an owner-only export path only when durable schema rollback is unsafe.

commands.invoke: dory sandbox run [options] -- COMMAND | dory sandbox create NAME [--workspace PATH] [--tool TOOL] [options] | dory sandbox exec NAME [options] -- COMMAND | dory sandbox reset|inspect|kill NAME | dory sandbox list
commands.isolation: dedicated doryd Linux VM per run
commands.networkPolicyNote: outbound permits Dory DNS plus resolved --allow-network destinations; all other IPv4/IPv6 destinations are denied for the workload uid.

agentReady.create: dory sandbox create NAME [--workspace PATH] [--tool TOOL] [options]

commands.notes: Runs commands in a dedicated VM and deletes it by default. Agent-ready named sandboxes keep prepared tools and caches between commands and can reset to a clean prepared baseline. No host files or credentials are visible unless explicitly granted.
Thin stdio Dory control server over the CLI command core, not a third-party MCP catalog or gateway. Launch with --read-only to block machine exec and sandbox writes.
